title: Child Abuse Prevention - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Child Care & Parenting > Child Abuse Prevention 70 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details ABC HOUSE CHILD ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assessments, support services, medical evaluations and forensic evaluations for suspected child abuse victims. Also provides off-site therapy and counseling referrals, and advocacy services. 228 SW 5th Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Children ages 0-18 who are affected by physical or sexual abuse, at risk of being abused, or have directly experienced abuse and their families. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm, by appointment only Email (541) 926-2203 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ABC HOUSE COMMUNITY EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a community outreach program that includes a variety of trainings. Programs for adult professionals and community members focusing on preventing, identifying, and responding to concerns of child abuse, child sexual abuse, and how to respond. Also offers a girls circle for adolescents to connect and share. Provides presentations for students in kindergarten through 12th grade, using an evidence-based curriculum that covers a variety of topics, such as how to prevent, recognize, and respond appropriately to bullying, cyberbullying, all types of abuse, and digital abuse dangers. Girls Circle is a peer support group that combines creative activity and facilitated discussion to build resiliency, self-efficacy, and self-esteem. 228 SW 5th Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Presentations: Unrestricted Girls Circle: At-risk girls ages 12-18 Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm by appointment only Girls Circle: 3-4 sessions per year with each session lasting 8-10 weeks. Meetings are held 1 day per week for 2 hours. Email (541) 926-2203 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AMANI CENTER CHILD ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assessments, support services, medical evaluations, and forensic evaluations for suspected child abuse victims. Also provides on-site and off-site therapy and counseling referrals, safety planning, advocacy services, and community training. Eligibility: Children ages 0-18 who are affected by physical or sexual abuse, at risk of being abused, or have directly experienced abuse. Families of these individuals are also eligible. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 366-4005 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BAY AREA HOSPITAL KIDS' HOPE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assessments, support services, medical evaluations and forensic evaluations. Offers advocacy services, off-site therapy and counseling referrals, community trainings, and prevention education. 1925 Thompson Road Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Ages 0-18 with suspected history of child abuse Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 226-8806 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CARES NORTHWEST CHILD ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assessment services for children with concerns of abuse or neglect, including medical examinations, forensic interviews, referral services, and trauma-focused therapy. Offers training, consultation, and referrals for professionals in the field. Also provides prevention education for professionals and the general public. 2800 N Vancouver Avenue Suite 201 Portland, OR 97227 Eligibility: Children ages 0-18 who are suspected victims of abuse, domestic violence, or neglect Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 276-9000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHILDREN'S ADVOCACY CENTER OF JACKSON COUNTY CHILD ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assessments, support services, medical evaluations, and forensic evaluations for suspected child abuse victims. Also provides therapy/counseling referrals, advocacy services, and community trainings. 816 W 10th Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 18 or younger who are alleged victims of child abuse; and non-offending family members Hours: Office Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Specialized therapy groups are scheduled at varying times. Call for information on days and times of parent groups. Email (541) 734-5437 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHILDREN'S ADVOCACY CENTER OF LINCOLN COUNTY CHILD ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assessments, support services, medical evaluations, and forensic evaluations for suspected child abuse victims. Also provides on-site and off-site therapy and counseling referrals, safety planning, advocacy services, and community training. 122 NE 47th Street Newport, OR 97365 Eligibility: Ages 3-18; may accommodate adults over age 18 with developmental disabilities Hours: Varies Email (541) 574-0841 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHILDREN'S CENTER OREGON CITY CHILD ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assessments, support services, medical evaluations and forensic evaluations for suspected child abuse victims. Also provides on-site therapy and off-site therapy/counseling referrals, advocacy services, community trainings, and prevention education. 1713 Penn Lane Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Ages 0-18 suspected to be victims of abuse Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 655-7725 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY CHILDREN'S COMMISSION HEALTHY FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Focuses on strengthening the parent-child relationship to assure healthy child growth and development. Home visitors support parents in cultivating and strengthening a nurturing, positive relationship with their baby at each visit. Parents receive information on topics like child development, infant care and keeping their baby healthy, and learning what is going on in their communities to support new families. A trained family support specialist engages with parents to nurture their baby in a safe and healthy environment that encourages learning. 16518 SE River Road Milwaukie, OR 97267 Eligibility: Families must score positive on the New Baby Questionnare needs assessment and then be enrolled during the prenatal through infant stages (90 days or younger) however once enrolled, children can remain enrolled until 36 months of age. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5:00pm. Evening hours are available for home visits as needed. Email (503) 675-4565 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION TEAM COMMUNITY ACTION TEAM CHILD AND FAMILY DEVELOPMENT NW OREGON HEALTHY FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides trained home visitors who coach families in parenting skills development. In order to lower the incidences of child abuse and neglect, increase effective parenting, and improve family health and well-being via parent education trainings, strengthening linkages among providers, and improving access to support for all families. Coordinated enrollment into subsidized preschool, including Head Start and Preschool Promise. Serves as the Coordinate Early Learning Hub for the county. 821 Main Street Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-8 Families at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level for subsidized preschool enrollment Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm Email (971) 288-8264 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILIES FIRST PARENT RESOURCE CENTER HEALTHY FAMILIES OREGON AND PARENTS AS TEACHERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Healthy Families Oregon and Parents As Teachers (PAT) programs provide information about a child’s growth, and what to expect from each stage of development. 401 S Canyon Boulevard John Day, OR 97845 Eligibility: Healthy Families Oregon: Low-income households Parents As Teachers (PAT): Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 575-1006 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY BUILDING BLOCKS CHELSEA'S PLACE RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers voluntary parenting support. The agency partners with parents by offering information about parenting and child development, a supportive professional who will listen and offer feedback, fun activities to do with your young child, therapeutic classrooms for children, connections with community resources, family nights and parent leadership opportunities. Offers classes for young children with trained teachers. 300 Jerry's Drive Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 673-4354 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY NURTURING CENTER FAMILY NURTURING CENTER JACKSON COUNTY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers comprehensive family support for families with children who are experiencing high levels of stress. Services include therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios, family support and individualized case management, home visiting, parenting education, and group activities designed to strengthen parenting skills and promote healthy child development 825 E Main Street Suite A Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Young children ages 0-5 who are experiencing high levels of stress or who have experienced trauma and/or are at risk for abuse and neglect; parents who have multiple risk factors and are struggling to nurture and care for their children. High-risk families typically experience several of the following factors: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single parenthood, criminal history, adult survivors of child abuse, history with drugs and alcohol, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, or social isolation. Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm Email (541) 779-5242 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY TREE RELIEF NURSERY ALBANY CLASSROOM FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves families with young children who are experiencing high levels of stress. Provides high-risk families with intensive support through individualized case management. Services include parenting education, support through home visits, group activities, and resource referral. Children participate in therapeutic classrooms with low adult-child ratios, and families receive home visits that promote healthy parenting and child development. 1055 Bain Street Southeast Albany, OR 97322 Eligibility: Birth-5 years old Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 967-6580 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY TREE RELIEF NURSERY FAMILY RELIEF NURSERY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Relief Nurseries serve families with young children who are experiencing high levels of stress. Family Tree Relief Nursery provides high-risk families with intensive support through individualized case management. Risk factors include: poverty, high stress, depression, history of mental illness, lack of reliable transportation, single parent, criminal history, adult victim of child abuse, drug and alcohol history, unemployment, low self-esteem, limited education, anger issues, homelessness, and social isolation. Services include parenting education, support through home visits, group activities, resource referral. Trained home visitors coach families in the development of parenting skills and support parents in cultivating and strengthening a nurturing, positive relationship with their baby at each visit. Parents receive information on topics like child development, infant care and keeping their baby healthy, and learn what is going on in their communities to support new families. 723 S Oregon Street Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Expectant families must enroll during pregnancy or before baby is 1 year old Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm; home visiting available some evenings Email (971) 222-5391 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMPACT NW IMPACT NW DANCING TREE RESOURCE CENTER COMMUNITIES FOR SAFE KIDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF An educational program that draws from the latest research on violence prevention. Providers go into local classrooms to teach children and youth how to get help if they are being abused or are living with violence. Offers programs to teach children that they have the right to be safe, to listen to their feelings, and to tell and keep telling safe adults when they need help. Also teaches adults (parents, caregivers, and teachers) how to listen to children, how to identify abuse indicators, and how to respond appropriately. 10055 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Grades K-5 Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 721-6792 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INSIGHTS YOUNG PARENT SERVICES HEALTHY FAMILIES OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Healthy Families Oregon focuses on strengthening the parent-child relationship to assure healthy child growth and development.
